chapter_title.ar: باب الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي يُوجِبُ التَّيَمُّمَ وَ مَنْ تَيَمَّمَ ثُمَّ وَجَدَ الْمَاءَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The timing which Obligates the Tayammum, and the one who performs Tayammum, then finds the water

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/180)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/180)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ لَا يَكُونُ مَعَهُ مَاءٌ وَ الْمَاءُ عَنْ يَمِينِ الطَّرِيقِ وَ يَسَارِهِ غَلْوَتَيْنِ أَوْ نَحْوَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ لَا آمُرُهُ أَنْ يُغَرِّرَ بِنَفْسِهِ فَيَعْرِضَ لَهُ لِصٌّ أَوْ سَبُعٌ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from Hammad Bin Usman, from Yaqoub Bin Salim who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who does not happen to have water with him, and the water is on the right of the road and the left, a couple of stone’s throw away or approximately that. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> do not order him that he should endanger himself, so a thief would present himself or a predator’.
en.sarwar: 8. Af-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from ai-Washsha’ from Hammad ibn ‘Uthman from Ya’qub ibn Salim who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who does not have any water (for Wudu’) during a journey, but there is water on the right or left side of the road at a distance of once or twice throwing of an arrow or so. He (the Imam) said, ‘I do not command him to mislead himself and then be endangered by the beasts or thieves.’”
